toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run these tests with `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host. 

For .NET, crash dump collection can be configured via environment variables. The standard approach is to use the `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump` environment variable (or `COMPlus_DbgEnableMiniDump` for older runtimes) along with related settings.

The key environment variables are:
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — enables crash dump collection
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType` — the type of dump (1=Mini, 2=Heap, 3=Triage, 4=Full)


- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName` — the path/filename for the dump

I need to set up the environment variables to capture a crash dump. I'll use a Heap dump (type 2) since it provides good diagnostic information without being as large as a full dump, then run the tests with these settings configured.

Actually, let me reconsider — a Full dump (type 4) would give me the most complete diagnostics if the test host crashes. I'll construct the command with all three environment variables: enabling minidumps, setting the type to Full, and specifying the output path with a process ID placeholder so each dump gets a unique filename.

Setting up the test command...

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-430b9d35c2044adebc2086062bd53656 && \\\n  DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \\\n  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \\\n  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=\"$PWD/crashdump_%p.dmp\" \\\n  dotnet test", "description": "Run dotnet test with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's the command used:

```bash
DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName="$PWD/crashdump_%p.dmp" \
dotnet test
```

**How it works:**
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — tells the .NET runtime to write a dump on unhandled crash
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` — captures a **full** dump (use `2` for heap-only if size is a concern)
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName` — sets the output path; `%p` is replaced with the crashing process's PID

No crashes occurred this run, so no dump was written. If a test ever crashes the host, a `.dmp` file will appear in the project directory.
